Temporo is an orchestrator, and you can write Temporo workflows for anything, so it's not meant for AI, so it was not designed for AI from the ground up, but it was designed for powering microservices on larger companies, where you have like an army of like smaller apps that have to talk to each other and they fail at different rates, they have different requirements and all that, and this just happens to be the perfect solution for be the for managing that, this just
APIs that will crash all the time, or they're unreliable, or that's just the nature of LLMs.

And can I do that first version as in natural language, since LLMs are getting so good at writing code.

And then on top of this, for anything that's LLM based, we think you need vertical interfaces.
If you want to do like high volume or repeatable work or high quality work, you can't rely on just like a chat interface.
Chat interface is great for like one offs.
So like if you want to do like write one article or if you want to do like one research.

And that thing has its own user interface with like different sub-agents, different features and all that.
Same thing if you want to do like a research product, then you have Perplexit.
In Perplexit, we have different buttons, have like different parts of the user, has a chat as part of the core there, but that chat does a lot more than just like freeform chat GPT.
And anything that's like freeform, you can't do like reliable, repeatable results.
So like, for example, if you're going to create articles, let's say you have 10,000 topics or like 1,000 topics that you need to write an article about how do you ensure the same steps, you know?
So that's where like guiding people.
As through this process, we think you need a dedicated user interface, most of the cases, and that is where the content OS part fits in.

Like that project, you need to come up with the strategy of what you write about, you need to come up with the opportunities that might be worth taking, and then once you take an opportunity, need to research in a certain way that's always as accurate as possible, and then you need to probably draft an outline, and then once you have the outline, you have to like create the content, and that's just for creating new content.
If you want to optimize the content, then there's like another set of things that you need to do, and like imagine doing that with a chat.
You can do it, but it depends on how you prompt the chat, depends how you structure the whole thing, depends on how you set up the project for that chat session, depends on a ton of stuff.
So that's where like we think.
Something like a dedicated user interface for the challenge that you're dealing with makes it doable for normal people to use.

For programmers, sure, you understand what a to-do list is, understand what a context window is, you understand what an API is, you can create a plan, and the agent under the hood is going to follow it.
For somebody that's a writer or an editor that doesn't understand how these things work under the hood, good luck.

So that is some of the challenge, I would think, like, vertical interface is still going to be super important, and then the last thing, the last part of the onion that we're thinking about, it's like, vertical interface is on top, ContentOS is an example of that, and then AI workflow engine in the middle, so we can build all the features that are AI powered, and then the last part would be, you're always going to need somebody to check on these things, it's like under-terministic, so you're going to have It can work, let's say you create the perfect workflow.

You create the perfect user interface on top, 95% of the time you click on the button and it works, the other 5%, maybe it's like, what the heck happened here, you know, like, we're like, it's like halfway there, or like, or like 80% there, but then you botched the last part of the work.

So we need to have ways to involve, or like ask for humans to unlock the workflows, or to train them, or to do the final, or to polish, or to make the decision of like, what is good and what is not good, and that is the part that is least developed on our system, like we haven't touched that at all, essentially.

Because, like today, everybody's combined, like writers, editors, everybody is like, they're joining the, they have to understand what the client does first, they have to onboard into the document, they have to be in Notion, they have to be in Slack, they have to be in a bunch of Zoom calls, just to get a sense for how to judge if that topic of choice is good or not, or if the draft is good or not, or like, if to fact check things properly.
So we think like we can make the entire delivery team on the writing team and editorial team and the research team to be a lot more efficient if we can create this like ad hoc market.

And Be you.

but the client would be the one choosing the strategy, so that's why we're trying to make the push for clients have access to the workspace, and they can pick the pages that they care about, pick the opportunities they care about, and ask the team, like, hey, generate this for me, you know, or, like, editorialize this for me, like, do the final pass, or, like, or write the entire from scratch, if it's, like, something that they want to do.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Onboard new engineers with a product overview and team process walkthrough.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=150.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Onboarding Path:** New engineers will spend 1–2 weeks on the Client Ops team to learn the service model before moving to product lanes (AI Framework, ContentOS, Check.ai).](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=108.0)
  - [**Product Strategy:** A three-layer stack is being built: an open-source AI Workflow Engine (Output) for code-first development, vertical interfaces (ContentOS, Check.ai) for repeatable tasks, and a future human-in-the-loop marketplace for quality control.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=2007.0)
  - [**Technical Roadmap:** The monolithic `Flow` repo will be migrated to the new `Output` framework, splitting into dedicated repos per product (Check.ai, ContentOS, GrowthX) to improve stability and prevent resource contention.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=4297.0)
  - [**Async-First Culture:** The team minimizes meetings via Slack, Linear, and Geekbot. Detailed Geekbot updates are critical for Daniel to generate leadership reports using Claude, making them a key communication channel.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=901.0)

## Topics

### Company Strategy & Product Vision

  - [**Service-to-Platform Model:** The company started as a service to manually close product gaps while building the platform. The goal is to shift clients to a self-serve model over time.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
  - [**Platform Architecture:** A three-layer stack is being built to enable scalable, high-quality AI content:](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=2007.0)
    1.  [**Vertical Interfaces:** Dedicated UIs (ContentOS, Check.ai) for repeatable, high-volume tasks that chat interfaces cannot handle reliably.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=1834.0)
    2.  [**AI Workflow Engine:** A code-first framework built on Temporal to manage unreliable LLM APIs. It will be open-sourced as **Output** to enable rapid prototyping and full code control.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)
    3.  [**Human-in-the-Loop Marketplace:** A future product to recruit, vet, and pay specialists for tasks like data labeling, fact-checking, and strategic input.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=2674.0)

### Product Deep Dive: ContentOS & Check.ai

  - [**ContentOS (fka Atlas):** The primary content marketing platform.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=3412.0)
      - [**Core Features:** Content Inventory (scrapes client sites), Context Artifacts (stores brand guidelines), and AI Pipelines (custom workflows).](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=2419.0)
      - [**Roadmap:** The Content Inventory and a revamped AI editor are the top priorities to enable client self-service.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=2520.0)
  - [**Check.ai:** A public index and private tracker for AI tool visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=2970.0)
      - [**Public Index:** Ranks companies (e.g., Copy.ai, ChatGPT) based on how often they appear in answers to common industry questions.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=3008.0)
      - [**Private Tracker:** Clients claim their company to track visibility against competitors and generate content roadmaps from a library of pre-researched prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=3145.0)

### Technical Roadmap: Migration to `Output` Framework

  - [**Current State:** The `Flow` repo is a monolithic codebase containing all workflows for Check.ai, ContentOS, and custom clients.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=3963.0)
  - [**Problem:** The monolith creates resource contention, where a large client job can degrade performance for everyone.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=4297.0)
  - [**Solution:** Migrate to the new `Output` framework, which simplifies workflow definition and enables splitting the codebase.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=4297.0)
      - [**New Structure:** The monolith will be split into dedicated repos per product (Check.ai, ContentOS, GrowthX), each with its own Temporal workspace.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=4297.0)
      - [**Benefit:** This isolation prevents resource contention and improves stability.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=4297.0)

### Team Processes & Tools

  - [**Slack:** The primary communication hub.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
      - [**Channel Prefixes:** `B-` (Build/Engineering) and `D-` (Delivery/Client Ops).](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
      - **Key Channels:** `#b-epd` (general engineering/product announcements), `#b-client-ops` (client tickets), and lane-specific channels (e.g., `#b-ai-framework`).
  - [**Linear:** The project management tool.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
      - [**Organization:** Kanban boards are used for most projects. The Client Ops team uses 2-week cycles to manage high-volume inbound requests.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [**Geekbot:** The daily async standup tool.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=901.0)
      - [**Purpose:** Provides a low-overhead way for Daniel to track progress and generate leadership reports using Claude, minimizing the need for status meetings.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Daniel:** Share the 4-hour feature-build video (Check.ai) with the new engineers.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
  - [**Daniel:** Create a dedicated onboarding channel for the new engineers.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=1170.0)
  - [**Isaac:** Connect with Daniel mid-week for an overview of Check.ai workflows and to begin contributing to a machine learning research study.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=4713.0)
  - [**All New Engineers:** Review the feature-build video to understand the stack and AI workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/4M3r8H_zkB4sXuJpYio8itiMwb5KvQbs?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
